questionHtml: Cardiac muscle cells and striated muscle fibres are highly specialized to produce movement. Compare and contrast the structure of both types of muscle cells.
markschemeHtml:
1. both contain myofibrils
    OR
    both have light and dark bands/ are striated;

  2. cardiac muscle cells are branched whereas striated muscle cells unbranched
    OR
    cardiac muscle cells Y shaped whereas striated muscle cells are elongated
    OR
    cardiac muscle cells are connected to other cardiac muscle cells but skeletal muscle cells are not;

  3. cardiac muscle cells are shorter than striated muscle cells;

  4. cardiac muscle cells have one nucleus but striated muscle cells have many nuclei/ are multinucleated;

meta.title: Cardiac muscle cells and striated muscle fibres are highly specialized to produce movement. Compare and contrast the structure of both types of muscle cells.
breadcrumbLabels: B2.3.9. Adaptations of cardiac muscle cells and striated muscle fibres
